item.title: California governor's race narrows as Hilton, Becerra take early lead
item.description: Steve Hilton, Xavier Becerra and Tom Steyer have emerged as the leading contenders to advance to November's general election, according to early counts.

item.title: Connie Chan and Scott Wiener advance in race to succeed Pelosi, CBS News projects
item.description: Voters in San Francisco on Tuesday advanced San Francisco Supervisor Connie Chan and state Sen. Scott Wiener in the race to succeed former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi in California's 11th Congressional District, CBS News projects.

item.title: Bessent testifies he told Pulte he was "going to kick his a**," not punch him
item.description: Bill Pulte, President Trump's pick for acting director of national intelligence, is being met with some skepticism on Capitol Hill.

item.title: Senate advances reconciliation bill, dropping ballroom funds ahead of final push
item.description: Senate Republicans moved forward with a package to fund the Department of Homeland Security's immigration agencies Wednesday.

item.title: Iran calls deadly attack on Kuwait retaliation for latest U.S. strikes
item.description: After Trump insisted talks with Iran were "going on continuously," Iran fired a deadly missile and drone salvo at Kuwait, claiming retaliation for new U.S. strikes.

item.title: Sam Forstag projected to win Democratic primary in Montana's 1st District
item.description: Democrats are hoping to pick up the open seat. GOP Rep. Ryan Zinke is retiring at the end of his term.

item.title: Ford recalls nearly 420,000 vehicles over faulty seat belts
item.description: Safety watchdog said seat belts in certain Ford Expedition and Lincoln vehicles may inadvertently lock, preventing them from functioning properly.

item.title: Grenell says he was "rattled" by sentencing of man convicted of threatening him
item.description: Ric Grenell, the former president of the Kennedy Center, gave a victim impact statement in court about the threats.

item.title: What travelers should know about Europe's new Entry/Exit System
item.description: Travel experts say to be prepared for potential disruptions as countries implement the new Entry/Exit System now in place across the EU and other countries.

item.title: Bitter GOP primary for South Dakota governor heads to runoff
item.description: Businessman and self-described outsider Toby Doeden and incumbent Gov. Larry Rhoden will advance to a runoff for the GOP nomination for South Dakota governor, CBS News projects.

item.title: California bank hostage situation ends after suspect shot dead
item.description: The suspect was pronounced dead at the scene early Wednesday morning, the Bakersfield Police Department said.

item.title: Here's where retirees should keep $20,000 right now (and where they shouldn't)
item.description: Not every savings account is suitable for retirees looking to protect $20,000 now. Here are two that are worth it.

item.title: These are the best airlines in 2026, according to The Points Guy
item.description: The Points Guy's annual ranking gives extra weight to affordability this year as higher fuel costs and airline fees squeeze travelers.

item.title: At least 21, mostly foreign nationals, dead in fire at India hotel
item.description: The fire started in the ground floor restaurant of an India hotel popular with patients at a nearby healthcare facility.

item.title: Lawmakers grill Rubio and Mullin on Capitol Hill
item.description: Lawmakers grilled Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin during tense congressional hearings on Capitol Hill. CBS News' Taurean Small has more.

item.title: 3 mortgage interest rate mistakes to avoid this June
item.description: There are some timely mortgage interest rate mistakes that borrowers will want to avoid making this June.

item.title: Deadly fire rips through New Delhi hotel
item.description: A deadly fire that broke out at the Flourish Inn Stay in southern Delhi's Malviya Nagar neighborhood killed at least 21 people, police said. CBS News' Shanelle Kaul has more.

item.title: Air traffic controller calls pilot "insane" after close call at Florida airport
item.description: Air traffic control also referred to the pilot of a small plane as "Mad Max."

item.title: 8 bagged bodies found with note in narco-trafficking hotspot
item.description: The discovery comes in the midst of a U.S.-backed military crackdown on the organized crime gangs in Ecuador.

item.title: Rubio on who won in Iran after he claimed war is over: "It's a fact"
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio continues to claim that the war in Iran is over during testimony before lawmakers on Capitol Hill. California Democratic Rep. Sara Jacobs asked Rubio about the war with Iran and who won based on Rubio's assessments.

item.title: Rubio spars with Rep. Lieu over claims of Trump sleeping at meetings, apparent cognitive decline
item.description: California Democratic Rep. Ted Lieu grilled Secretary of State Marco Rubio about President Trump's health, showing video at a House Foreign Affairs Committee hearing of past meetings in which Mr. Trump appears to be asleep. Lieu also asked Rubio about the president's health after his repeated medical visits.

item.title: Watch: Rubio says he won't talk about Israel's nuclear capacities in public hearing
item.description: Texas Democratic Rep. Joaquin Castro asked Secretary of State Marco Rubio to expand on Israel's nuclear capacities during a House Foreign Affairs Committee hearing on Thursday, as the Iran war rages on and as Israel continues to strike Hezbollah in Lebanon.

item.title: Social Security checks could be cut by $500 a month in 2032, report finds
item.description: Beneficiaries would continue receiving payments if Social Security's trust fund is depleted, but checks could shrink by about 24%, according to a new report.

item.title: Scientists find yeast in frozen mummy's guts, use it to make bread
item.description: More than 5,300 years ago, Oetzi the Iceman was strolling through the Alps on the border of Austria and Italy when he was killed by an arrow in the back.

item.title: Watch: Senator grills Rubio about funds for Trump's "Board of Peace"
item.description: Nevada Democratic Sen. Jacky Rosen pressed Secretary of State Marco Rubio about money going into at least two bank accounts to fund President Trump's "Board of Peace" initiative in the Middle East.

item.title: Watch: Van Hollen slams "illegal war" in Iran and calls Trump foreign policy a "dumpster fire"
item.description: Maryland Democratic Sen. Chris Van Hollen slammed Secretary of State Marco Rubio during a Senate Foreign Relations Committee hearing over the Trump administration's war in Iran and denounced changes to foreign aid policies and other matters overseas.

item.title: Watch: Rubio says there's indication that Iran Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is alive
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio testified before the Senate Foreign Relations Committee on Tuesday and said that there are indications that Iran's Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, is alive, expanding on the communication methods being used to negotiate with Tehran.

item.title: Rubio says Trump started Iran war because of buildup of missiles, drones to defend nuclear hopes
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is testifying before the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, where he discussed the Iran war and the apparent military buildup that led to Operation Epic Fury in the Middle East. This comes as the Trump administration faces more backlash for the prolonged conflict.

item.title: Marco Rubio testifies on Capitol Hill, says U.S. is "not a charity" and "we are here to win"
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is facing lawmakers on Capitol Hill to make the case for his department's 2027 budget. During his opening statement, Rubio touted his efforts with the Trump administration, reiterated the recent U.S. shifts on foreign aid and discussed negotiations with Iran.

item.title: Teen released by ICE wants to stay in U.S.: "Doing the right things"
item.description: Ricardo Hernandez-Navarrete graduated from high school after being released by ICE, but he and his mother still face the possibility of deportation.
